summary: Proposed adding generated Ninja targets `format` and `format-check` that invoke a new `tools/format.py` wrapper around `git clang-format`, plus a CONTRIBUTING.md update directing contributors to those targets. The PR was closed unmerged after feedback questioned the utility of wrapping the entire formatting operation in Python instead of using Ninja to track per-file formatting work or documenting the existing pre-commit setup.
what_changed: Added a new `tools/format.py` script that runs `git clang-format` from the Git worktree root.
The script accepted an optional base revision, `--check`, and `--clang-format` arguments.
The script checked for missing `clang-format` and `git-clang-format` executables and printed direct errors rather than tracebacks.
For check mode, the wrapper passed `--diff` to `git clang-format` and returned failure if stdout contained a `diff --git` block.
Updated `tools/project.py` to emit Ninja rules named `clang_format` and `clang_format_check` using `$python tools/format.py` and `$python tools/format.py --check`.
Updated `tools/project.py` to emit phony-like build outputs `format` and `format-check` with implicit dependencies on `tools/format.py` and `.clang-format`.
Changed CONTRIBUTING.md auto-formatting guidance from using `git clang-format` on staged files to running `ninja format` or `ninja format-check` after configuring the project.
smart_moves: Preserved the repository's existing changed-line formatting behavior by delegating to `git clang-format` instead of attempting a whole-repository format.
Included `format-check` as a non-mutating workflow suitable for local validation or CI-style checks.
Made the Ninja targets depend implicitly on the wrapper script and `.clang-format`, so generated build metadata could notice changes to formatting configuration.
Validated both direct script use and generated Ninja targets locally, including missing-tool and outside-worktree error paths.
decomp_lessons: For decomp projects, formatting automation should avoid reformatting large unmatched or legacy areas; changed-line workflows reduce noisy diffs.
However, a Ninja target that shells out to a global `git clang-format` operation may not take advantage of Ninja's incremental dependency tracking.
When adding developer workflow tooling, consider whether the build system is the right integration point or whether existing hooks such as pre-commit already solve the workflow better.
Documentation changes that redirect contributor behavior should match the accepted tooling strategy; this PR's doc update became questionable once the implementation approach was challenged.
naming_conventions: Used user-facing Ninja target names `format` and `format-check`.
Used internal Ninja rule names `clang_format` and `clang_format_check`.
Used Python helper names `git_root` and `run_git_clang_format`, which directly describe their wrapper responsibilities.
Kept the wrapper script under `tools/format.py`, consistent with project tooling placement.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No assembly or code-matching tactics were involved; this PR was limited to formatting workflow/tooling.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Questioned the utility of the implementation because issue #1543 was intended to run each file through `clang-format` individually so Ninja can track dirty files and format efficiently.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Argued that outsourcing the whole formatting operation to `git-clang-format` through a Python script was less compelling than a commit hook.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Pointed out that pre-commit was already set up through `.pre-commit-config.yaml` and suggested documenting pre-commit setup and possibly adding it to `requirements.in`.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that implement per-file Ninja clang-format tracking.
Find PRs that document or require pre-commit setup for formatting.
Search for changes to `.pre-commit-config.yaml` or `requirements.in` after PR #2451.
Check whether issue #1543 was later closed by a different formatting workflow implementation.
